stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • “The Lesson” is a short story.
  • It was written by African American author Toni Cade Bambara.
  • It was first published in her book Gorilla, My Love in 1972.
  • It is narrated by a young girl named Sylvia.
  • It takes place primarily in New York City.
stem: The student wants to indicate where the short story takes place. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: “The Lesson” was written by Toni Cade Bambara.
“The Lesson” is narrated by a young girl named Sylvia.
“The Lesson” was published in Gorilla, My Love.
“The Lesson” takes place primarily in New York City.

rationale: Choice D is the best answer because it states where the story takes place. Choice A is incorrect because it identifies the author, not the setting. Choice B is incorrect because it identifies the narrator, not the setting. Choice C is incorrect because it identifies the book in which the story appeared, not where the story occurs.
